data.description:
# Discord Link Aggregation Bot

> **Summary**: A TypeScript Discord bot that monitors a channel for shared links, stores them in PostgreSQL with pgvector, generates LLM summaries and embeddings, and enables natural language semantic search to rediscover content.

## Problem Statement

Users share valuable links in Discord channels, but these links are easily lost in chat history and difficult to rediscover later. There is no way to search or retrieve previously shared content by topic or context.

## Users

**Primary User: Community Members**
- As a community member, I want to share a link in Discord and have it automatically catalogued so that I don't need to manually organize or tag my shares.
- As a community member, I want to ask natural language questions about previously shared content so that I can rediscover relevant links without scrolling through chat history.
- As a community member, I want to see a brief summary and preview image of stored links so that I can quickly assess relevance before clicking.

**Secondary User: Server Administrators**
- As a server admin, I want the bot to gracefully handle downtime and catch up on missed messages so that no shared links are lost.
- As a server admin, I want the bot to monitor a configurable channel (initially single, expandable later) so that I can control which conversations are indexed.

## Success Criteria

1. **Link Detection & Storage**: Bot detects all URLs in monitored channel messages and creates/updates records in PostgreSQL with pgvector storage within 30 seconds of message posting.
2. **Content Summarization**: Each stored link includes an LLM-generated summary of the source content, produced via custom OpenAI-compatible LLM proxy with configurable model.
3. **Image Extraction**: Bot extracts Open Graph or meta images from linked pages to provide visual previews.
4. **Semantic Search**: Users can query stored content using natural language, with results returned via semantic similarity search using vector embeddings.
5. **Downtime Recovery**: On startup, bot identifies the last processed Discord message and processes all subsequent messages before resuming real-time monitoring.
6. **Query Detection**: Bot automatically detects when messages appear to be questions about stored content and responds in the same channel.
7. **Duplicate Handling**: When the same URL is posted again, the existing record is updated rather than creating duplicates.
8. **Error Feedback**: Failed link processing is indicated via emoji reaction on the original message.
9. **Configuration**: Bot configuration (Discord token, channel ID, LLM proxy URL, model name, database connection) is managed via environment variables or config file.

## Constraints

- **Technical**:
  - TypeScript with discord.js framework
  - PostgreSQL with pgvector extension for vector storage
  - Custom LLM proxy with OpenAI-compatible API (not Ollama directly)
  - Local development environment only (no containerization initially)
  - Must use established article extraction library (e.g., @extractus/article-extractor or mozilla/readability)
  
- **Operational**:
  - Initially monitors a single channel (architecture should support future multi-channel expansion)
  - Embeddings generated via the same custom LLM proxy
  - Query access open to all users in the channel

## Existing State

This is a greenfield project with no existing codebase. The repository currently contains only workflow documentation (AGENTS.md) and worklog configuration.

## Desired Change

Build a complete Discord bot application that:

1. **Discord Integration Layer**
   - Connect to Discord using discord.js
   - Monitor designated channel for new messages
   - Implement startup recovery to process missed messages
   - Respond to queries in-channel with natural language detection

2. **Content Processing Pipeline**
   - Extract URLs from message content
   - Fetch and parse linked pages using article extraction library
   - Extract Open Graph/meta images
   - Generate summaries via LLM proxy
   - Generate embeddings via LLM proxy

3. **Data Storage Layer**
   - PostgreSQL database schema with pgvector extension
   - JSONB storage for flexible metadata
   - Track last processed Discord message ID for recovery
   - Upsert logic for duplicate URL handling

4. **Query/Retrieval System**
   - Natural language query detection in chat messages
   - Semantic search using pgvector similarity
   - Optional hybrid search (semantic + keyword) if beneficial
   - Format and return results in Discord

## Related Work

- No related work items found in worklog (this is the first work item: SB-0MN1L2P9Z03HOQJN)
- No existing Discord bot code in repository
- No existing database schemas or configurations
- Greenfield repository initialized for this project

## Risks & Assumptions

**Risks:**
- **Rate Limiting**: Discord and external websites may rate-limit requests; mitigation: implement backoff and queuing.
- **Content Extraction Failures**: Some sites may block scraping, have paywalls, or dynamic content; mitigation: graceful error handling with emoji feedback.
- **LLM Proxy Availability**: Custom LLM proxy downtime could block processing; mitigation: queue failed items for retry.
- **Query Detection Accuracy**: Automatic question detection may have false positives/negatives; mitigation: tune detection logic, consider adding explicit command fallback later.
- **Scope Creep**: Feature expansion (multi-channel, web UI, etc.) could delay MVP; mitigation: record enhancement ideas as separate work items linked to this epic rather than expanding scope.
- **Discord API Changes**: Discord may change API or rate limits; mitigation: use stable discord.js version, monitor changelog.
- **Large Message Backlog**: Extended downtime could result in many messages to process on startup; mitigation: implement batching with progress logging.

**Assumptions:**
- Custom LLM proxy is already operational and accessible
- PostgreSQL with pgvector extension is available locally
- Discord bot token and permissions will be provided
- Article extraction libraries will handle majority of web content types

## Appendix: Intake Interview Q&A

### Round 1: Core Technology Choices

**Q: What types of links should the bot process? Should it handle all URLs, or only specific types (e.g., articles, YouTube videos, GitHub repos)?**
A: All URLs

**Q: Which LLM provider should be used for generating summaries?**
A: Local/self-hosted

**Q: What programming language/framework do you want to use for the bot?**
A: TypeScript (discord.js)

### Round 2: Features & Scope

**Q: How should the representative image be created? The description mentions 'an image to represent it'.**
A: Extract from page metadata

**Q: How will users query the bot to retrieve content lists? Through Discord commands, a web interface, or both?**
A: Natural language in Discord

**Q: Should the bot monitor a single channel, multiple specific channels, or all channels it has access to?**
A: Initially a single channel, but this may change in the future.

### Round 3: Infrastructure & Data Handling

**Q: For the local LLM, which Ollama model should be used? Or should it be configurable?**
A: It won't be Ollama, it is my custom LLM proxy which uses the OpenAI API. The model used will be configurable.

**Q: What deployment approach should be used? Docker Compose, Kubernetes, or local development only?**
A: Local dev only

**Q: How should the bot handle duplicate links (same URL posted multiple times)?**
A: Update existing record

### Round 4: Search & Access

**Q: How should the pgvector capability be used? For semantic similarity search via embeddings, keyword search, or both?**
A: I certainly want semantic search. If there is benefit in having keyword search as well then both.

**Q: When a user queries for content, where should the results be displayed?**
A: Reply in same channel

**Q: Who should be able to query the bot for stored content?**
A: Open to all users

### Round 5: Implementation Details

**Q: For generating vector embeddings, should the bot use your custom LLM proxy (OpenAI-compatible), a local embedding model, or should this be configurable?**
A: Custom LLM proxy

**Q: How should the bot extract content from linked pages for summarization? Basic HTML scraping, reader-mode extraction, or content-type aware handling?**
A: Are there suitable libraries that cover this already? If so use one (Note: @extractus/article-extractor or mozilla/readability recommended)

**Q: When the bot fails to process a link (site unreachable, paywall, etc.), how should it handle the error?**
A: Emoji reaction on failure

### Round 6: Query Interaction

**Q: For natural language queries, how should the bot know when a message is a query vs. regular conversation? Should users mention the bot, use a keyword, or should it try to detect questions automatically?**
A: Automatic detection

data.description:
Problem statement

Discord-shared YouTube links are not being ingested correctly. We need reliable extraction and storage of YouTube video metadata (title, description, thumbnail), optional transcripts, LLM-generated summaries, and embeddings so video content becomes searchable and summarizable like other link types.

Users

- Primary: Community members who share YouTube links and later want to rediscover or search video content.
  - As a community member, when I post or see a YouTube link, I want it indexed with a short summary and searchable embeddings so I can find it later by topic.
- Secondary: Server operators/administrators who want ingestion to be robust and respectful of rate limits and API quotas.
  - As an operator, I want ingest behavior to fail gracefully, log useful diagnostics, and support backfill of missing embeddings or transcripts.

Success criteria

- New YouTube link ingestion records are created on detection and persist video metadata (video id, title, description, thumbnail URL) within 30s of message processing.
- LLM-generated summary and vector embedding are produced and stored for every ingested video; missing embeddings are queued for backfill and completed within configured SLA (example: 24h) for backlog items.
- Transcripts/captions are fetched when available (or via light-weight caption API) and stored; when transcript retrieval is enabled, summaries reflect transcript content.
- Tests cover detection, normalization (YouTube URL -> canonical video id), metadata extraction, summary/embedding generation, and backfill behavior.

Constraints

- Use existing ingestion abstractions and LLM/embedding pipeline (src/ingestion/*, src/llm/*, PostgreSQL+pgvector) to avoid large refactors.
- External APIs (YouTube data/captions) impose rate limits and may require API keys; design must allow configurable API credentials, rate limiting and graceful retry/backoff.
- Storage cost for transcripts and embeddings should be considered (configurable toggle for transcript storage vs. summary-only indexing).

Existing state

- Ingestion pipeline and LLM/embedding generation exist (see src/ingestion/service.ts, src/llm/*, README.md) and handle web page links and other content types.
- There is an existing work item: "Ingest Yout Tube links" (SB-0MN1Z8HBC03YSOJ9) created as an idea; console logs show a failure when handling YouTube links.
- Tests and helpers for ingestion are present (tests/ingestion.service.test.ts, tests/ingestion.url.test.ts) and should be extended for YouTube-specific flows.

Desired change

- Detect YouTube URLs during message ingestion, normalize to canonical video id, and create an ingestion record linking to that id.
- Fetch video metadata (title, description, thumbnail, channel) via YouTube API or safe HTML fallback, store metadata in the existing DB schema alongside other link records.
- If captions/transcripts are available (via YouTube API or light-weight caption fetch), store transcripts and use them as input to LLM summarization and embedding. If unavailable, fall back to metadata-based summary.
- Produce an LLM summary and vector embedding using the existing LLM/embedding pipeline; queue missing embeddings/transcripts for background backfill.
- Add tests and docs; expose config flags for enabling transcript fetching and API credentials.

Related work

- Potentially related docs:
  - README.md — high-level ingestion architecture and components (doc)
  - src/ingestion/service.ts — ingestion orchestration (code)
  - src/ingestion/url.ts — URL extraction and normalization utilities (code)
  - src/llm/embeddings.ts, src/llm/client.ts — existing LLM/embedding integrations (code)
  - tests/ingestion.service.test.ts, tests/ingestion.url.test.ts — existing tests to extend (tests)

- Potentially related work items:
  - Build link ingestion and content extraction pipeline (SB-0MN1MK6YG1L59RGS) — implements core ingestion pipeline used by other link types
  - Add crawl command handling for linked pages (SB-0MN2BXQDI0AUHU3M) — related to multi-page crawling and discovery
  - Report on page processing progress (SB-0MN2CJF990ZLPVC1) — progress-reporting behavior to apply across ingestion flows
  - Make crawler better behaved (SB-0MN2C45HA1O839C4) — crawl politeness and robots handling (constraints to respect)
  - Enable the ingestion of PDF content (SB-0MN2W9WNY0JTX1PZ) — similar content-type ingestion concerns

Notes / open questions

- Confirm whether a YouTube Data API key will be provided, or if we should implement an HTML/meta scrape fallback first.
- Confirm expected transcript source: prefer YouTube captions via API (if available) or a separate speech-to-text job (costly) — user selected transcripts in success criteria, please confirm acceptable source and SLA.

Responses received:

- YouTube Data API key will be provided.
- Transcript source: YouTube captions via API; backlog backfill within 24h.

Please review this draft and tell me if you want changes or if I should proceed to finalize it for SB-0MN1Z8HBC03YSOJ9.
data.title: Ingest Yout Tube links

data.description:
Implement handling for messages like 'Crawl http://...'.

Goal:
When a crawl instruction is received, fetch the target page and then fetch all pages linked from the main body/content region of that page. Each fetched page should be processed as a separate entry into the database.

User story:
As an operator issuing crawl instructions, I want one command to ingest a seed page and its in-body linked pages so that related content is captured in one pass.

Expected behavior:
- Detect messages in the form 'Crawl <URL>' (or equivalent phrasing already handled by parser/router).
- Retrieve the seed URL.
- Extract links from the main body/content area (not global nav/footer/chrome where feasible in current architecture).
- Resolve relative links to absolute URLs.
- Fetch linked pages with sensible safeguards (dedupe, skip invalid/unfetchable URLs).
- Process every successfully fetched page as an individual DB entry.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Given a crawl message with a valid URL, the system fetches the seed page.
2) Links from the seed page main body are discovered and normalized.
3) Seed page plus each discovered linked page are processed into DB entries.
4) Duplicate URLs are not processed more than once in a single crawl request.
5) Failures on some linked pages do not prevent processing others; errors are surfaced/logged.
6) Automated tests cover link extraction/normalization and multi-page ingestion flow.

Notes:
- Keep implementation aligned with existing ingestion and storage abstractions.
- discovered-from: user request in current session
data.title: Add crawl command handling for linked pages

data.description: Improve the crawl feature of this bot by making it respect robots.txt and adding delays between retrieval. If possible use a crawler library rather than implementing this yourself.
data.title: Make crawler better behaved

data.description:
Bug discovered while validating Add crawl command handling for linked pages (SB-0MN2BXQDI0AUHU3M).

Observed behavior:
A message 'crawl https://howtomarketagame.com/blog/' only ingests the seed URL and does not ingest linked pages.

Expected behavior:
When crawl seed is processed, linked pages from the main body should be discovered and ingested even if extractor-provided link list is empty.

Likely cause:
Some pages return extracted body content without populating extractor links[] metadata. Current crawl discovery relies primarily on links[].

Plan:
- Add fallback link extraction from extracted body HTML anchor tags.
- Normalize/dedupe and merge extractor links[] + body-derived links.
- Add tests covering empty links[] with body anchors.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Crawl plan discovers links when extractor links[] is empty but content contains anchor hrefs.
2) Seed + discovered pages are ingested as separate entries.
3) Existing URL extraction behavior remains unchanged.
4) Tests pass.

related-to:SB-0MN2BXQDI0AUHU3M
data.title: Fix crawl fallback link discovery from extracted body HTML

data.description:
# Headline
Add in-channel progress reporting for link ingestion by posting one bot status message and editing it as work advances. Apply this to all ingestion paths, with per-URL updates and crawl progress counts.

## Problem statement
Operators currently receive only reaction-based signals during ingestion, which do not clearly communicate current progress for longer crawls or multi-URL ingestion runs. This makes it hard to tell what the bot is doing, how far it has progressed, and whether it is stalled.

## Users
- Discord channel operators who submit single-link or crawl ingestion commands.
- Team members monitoring bot behavior during long-running ingestion.

User stories:
- As an operator, I want to see one status message updated in place so channel noise stays low while I can still track ingestion progress.
- As an operator, I want crawl progress shown as `x/y` so I can estimate remaining work.
- As an operator, I want per-URL status text so I can identify which URL is currently being processed.

## Success criteria
- For each ingestion-triggering message, the bot posts a single status message and edits that same message throughout processing.
- The behavior applies to all link ingestion flows (single URL and crawl flows).
- For crawl flows, status updates include a running `x/y` count across URLs processed.
- Status updates are per-URL and reflect meaningful processing phases (for example: fetching, extracting links, storing).
- On completion, the status message is updated to a terminal state indicating success, partial success, or failure.

## Constraints
- Keep channel noise low by using one editable status message rather than many progress messages.
- Preserve existing ingestion behavior and error handling; this item is focused on progress visibility, not ingestion logic changes.
- Work within current Discord API/message capabilities in the existing bot architecture.

## Existing state
- `src/index.ts` manages monitored-message handling and currently uses reactions (`👀`, `🤔`, and configured success/failure reactions) as lightweight progress signals.
- `src/ingestion/service.ts` executes the ingest pipeline for URLs and crawl-discovered pages but does not expose user-visible step-by-step text progress.
- No in-place edited status message is currently produced for ingestion operations.

## Desired change
- Introduce a progress-reporting path for ingestion that sends one status message and updates it in place as ingestion progresses.
- Emit per-URL progress updates for all ingestion flows.
- For crawls, include a running `x/y` progress counter as discovered URLs are processed.
- Keep existing reactions unless implementation constraints require adjustment.

## Related work
- Add crawl command handling for linked pages (SB-0MN2BXQDI0AUHU3M): Parent crawl feature where user-visible crawl progress naturally fits.
- Fix crawl fallback link discovery from extracted body HTML (SB-0MN2C6W8Y13RVKWI): Recent crawl behavior work that influences discovered-URL processing.
- `src/index.ts`: Entry point for message handling and likely place to initiate/manage status-message lifecycle.
- `src/ingestion/service.ts`: Core per-URL ingestion orchestration and best place to emit progress events.
- `src/discord/client.ts`: Discord client abstraction relevant if progress update hooks are added at interface boundaries.

## Related work (automated report)
- Add crawl command handling for linked pages (SB-0MN2BXQDI0AUHU3M): This in-progress parent feature already defines crawl ingestion behavior across seed and discovered pages, so progress messaging should align with its processing flow and error tolerance expectations.
- Fix crawl fallback link discovery from extracted body HTML (SB-0MN2C6W8Y13RVKWI): This completed item changed how crawl URLs are discovered from extracted HTML content, which directly affects how many URLs can appear in `x/y` progress counters during crawl runs.
- `src/index.ts`: The monitored-message orchestrator currently drives reactions and invokes ingestion; it is the natural place to create a single status message and manage its lifecycle per user request.
- `src/ingestion/service.ts`: The service already has clear per-URL processing stages (fetch/extract/summarize/embed/store) and is the best source of structured progress events used to edit the status message.
- `README.md`: Existing docs describe reaction-based ingestion signals and should be updated to document in-place status message edits and crawl progress counting behavior.

## Risks and assumptions
- Assumption: A single editable status message per ingestion request is acceptable UX for both short and long ingestion runs.
- Assumption: Existing reaction-based signals remain in place unless implementation constraints require changing them.
- Risk: Message edit rate limits may affect very frequent updates; mitigation is to keep update cadence aligned to meaningful stage transitions.
- Risk: Partial URL failures in a crawl can lead to misleading final status text; mitigation is to explicitly represent partial success in terminal status.
- Risk: Scope creep into broader UX redesign (threads, rich embeds, dashboards); mitigation is to capture extra UX ideas as separate linked work items instead of expanding this item.

data.author: opencode
data.comment: Implemented crawl instruction support for messages like 'Crawl <url>'. The ingestion flow now builds a plan from the crawl seed, extracts linked pages from the seed article body links, normalizes and deduplicates discovered URLs, and ingests seed + linked pages as separate DB entries while continuing on per-page failures. Updated files: src/ingestion/service.ts, src/ingestion/url.ts, src/ingestion/extractor.ts, tests/ingestion.service.test.ts, tests/ingestion.url.test.ts. Validation: npm test (26 tests passed).

data.author: opencode
data.comment: Implemented crawl politeness safeguards: added robots.txt-aware crawl policy with user-agent-specific allow/disallow parsing and crawl-delay support, integrated it into ingestion so seed/discovered URLs are checked before fetch, and applied per-host pacing fallback delay when robots rules are absent. Updated configuration/docs with CRAWL_USER_AGENT and CRAWL_DELAY_MS and added tests for crawl policy behavior and ingestion skip-on-blocked-link flow. Validation: npm run lint and npm run test both pass (32 tests).

data.author: opencode
data.comment: Fixed crawl discovery fallback: when extractor-provided links[] is empty, ingestion now extracts anchor hrefs from extracted HTML content and merges them with links[] before normalization/deduplication. Updated files: src/ingestion/url.ts, src/ingestion/service.ts, tests/ingestion.url.test.ts, tests/ingestion.service.test.ts. Validation: npm test (28 tests passed).

data.author: opencode
data.comment: Implemented progress reporting for ingestion by adding callback-based progress events in IngestionService (downloading/extracting_links/storing/completed), including crawl x/y counters and final run result summary. Wired a single in-place edited Discord status message in index.ts so each ingest request posts one status message and updates it throughout processing. Added tests covering crawl per-URL progress events and counters in tests/ingestion.service.test.ts. Validation: npm run lint and npm run test pass (33 tests).
